reasoning: Federal current expenditures (FGEXPND) are given as an annualized figure. Even with aggressive cuts led by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), a $250 billion year-over-year reduction in nominal government expenditures between Q4 2024 and Q4 2025 is historically very difficult to achieve. Most of the federal budget is composed of mandatory spending (like Social Security and Medicare) and interest on the debt, both of which generally increase over time. While Elon Musk and DOGE may push for substantial operational and discretionary cuts, realizing a massive $250 billion net reduction in a single year across total structural base spending would be nearly unprecedented. Therefore, I estimate a low probability.
